hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) electrostatic interactions and improve selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating candidates with strong SSTR2 binding but insufficient selectivity. The focus must shift to explicitly disrupting conserved off-target interactions while preserving the pharmacophore and cysteine constraints.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT at 7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.
Introducing mutations at key non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining native-like interactions with SSTR2.
